Initialization and finalization functions for ECMA components.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that declares two external functions, [`ecma_init`](<#ecma_init>) and [`ecma_finalize`](<#ecma_finalize>). These functions are responsible for the initialization and finalization of ECMA components within a software system. The header file uses include guards, defined by `ECMA_INIT_FINALIZE_H`, to prevent multiple inclusions of the same file, which can cause compilation errors. The file is part of a larger module related to ECMA, as indicated by the grouping comments, and it provides the necessary declarations for managing the lifecycle of ECMA components.
# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### ecma\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ecma_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/base/ecma-init-finalize.h#L26>)

Initializes ECMA components for use.
- **Description**: Use this function to prepare ECMA components before any operations that depend on them. It must be called once before using any other ECMA-related functions to ensure proper setup of internal structures and memory management. This function registers necessary callbacks and initializes global environments, which are essential for the correct functioning of the ECMA components.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`ecma_init`](<ecma-init-finalize.c.md#ecma_init>)  (Implementation)


---
### ecma\_finalize<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ecma_finalize}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/base/ecma-init-finalize.h#L27>)

Finalizes ECMA components and releases resources.
- **Description**: Use this function to clean up and release resources associated with ECMA components when they are no longer needed. It must be called to ensure that all memory and resources are properly freed, preventing memory leaks. This function should be called after all ECMA operations are complete and before the program terminates.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`ecma_finalize`](<ecma-init-finalize.c.md#ecma_finalize>)  (Implementation)
